seoTitle: Lower Norton vs Loy Yang — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lower Norton and Loy Yang. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lower Norton vs Loy Yang: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Norton and Loy Yang.
heading: Lower Norton vs Loy Yang — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Norton, Victoria and Loy Yang, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Norton has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Loy Yang (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Norton or Loy Yang safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Norton has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Loy Yang scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Norton scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Loy Yang scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Norton or Loy Yang?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Norton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
